name: Biscoff Protein Cheesecake
description: Biscoff cookie-butter protein cheesecake, crustless - a flavor variant of the Vanilla Protein Cheesecake base, built from pantry staples (no cookie-butter emulsion, no fat-free cream cheese, no powdered PB, no brown butter). Keeps the base's PROVEN dairy ratio (light cream cheese 113 g + cottage 57 g + egg white 30 g - the same ratio as the excellent-rated Creme Brulee variant) for a dense, creamy set; 55 g Biscoff (40 g batter + 15 g swirl) plus a toasted speculoos spice blend (cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg) carry the cookie flavor. ~168 cal/slice, ratio ~11.8 - a bit richer than the leanest build, but texture is the point and it's still well under the Turtle (215). To-make.

payload.instructionText: Melt 15 g Biscoff 10-15 sec until pourable and drizzle it over the top of the batter. Drag a toothpick through in a few S-curves to marble it - swirl, don't stir it in.

payload.text: The cooked pumpkin must be at room temperature before it goes in - even slightly warm, it starts cooking the eggs and leaves scrambled streaks in the finished cheesecake.
Blend the cottage cheese completely smooth - any visible curd survives baking and turns the cheesecake grainy. An immersion blender in a tall cup works; a food processor needs a full 2 minutes.

Toast the ground cinnamon in a dry pan 30-60 sec until fragrant before it goes in - straight from the jar it tastes flat; toasting blooms its oils and is what makes the spice read as speculoos against the Biscoff. Stir the ginger and nutmeg pinches in off the heat.
Don't overbeat once the eggs are in - mix on low just until smooth. Beating in air makes a lean cheesecake puff, then crack and sink, and it won't set to the dense texture this is tuned for.
A protein cheesecake sets firmer and dries out faster than a full-fat one - do NOT overbake. Pull at a just-set center with a slight wobble; chase a fully firm center and it bakes out rubbery.
Swirl the melted Biscoff on top, don't stir it in - a few toothpick S-curves keep a marbled pattern. Blend it all the way in and you just muddy the color and lose the 'Biscoff cheesecake' look.

Crustless by design - the Biscoff and toasted spices carry the cookie flavor without a crust's calories. If you want a crust, the Protein Graham Crust sub-recipe slots in like it does on the other cheesecakes (about +50 cal/slice).

payload.instructionText: Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add the yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, the 40 g Biscoff, the toasted cinnamon (toast it in a dry pan 30-60 sec until fragrant first), a pinch each of ground ginger and nutmeg, and the vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined - don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.
